name: IMANA
mission: IMANA FOSTERS HEALTH PROMOTION, DISEASE PREVENTION, AND HEALTH MAINTENANCE IN COMMUNITIES AROUND THE WORLD THROUGH DIRECT PATIENT CARE, HEALTH PROGRAMS, AND ADVOCACY.
theoryOfChange: The 'Nafis Salaam' program, a partnership between the Muslim Consultative Network (MCN) and the Islamic Medical Association of North America (IMANA), utilized a logic model. This model emphasized faith-based messaging and religious motivators to help participants quit tobacco use. The program's approach is detailed in an article published on jima.imana.org.
scoreSummary: Islamic Medical Association of North America shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Accreditation as a Provider of Continuing Medical Education (ACCME)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://imana.org/donate (Source: https://imana.org/donate)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Strong donor fit, low risk profile, and credible supporting evidence for outcomes.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 33 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 46/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.evidence: Beneficiary count appears implausible; CPB excluded pending review
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Separate restricted/endowment funds from operating reserves and publish a clear reserve-use policy.

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Medical Association of North America shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://imana.org/donate (Source: https://imana.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: IMANA holds a 99.75/100 rating and maintains a program expense ratio of 68.7% and 33.0 months of working capital.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and provides specific policies for Zakat distribution.
The organization reported total revenue of $3,675,972 and a fundraising efficiency of $0.10 per $1 raised.
IMANA offers various donation options and giving methods for its global medical relief programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMANA earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial accountability and strong alignment with Muslim donor values, though its impact score reflects a need for more granular cost-effectiveness data.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing the program expense ratio, which currently stands at 68.7%
Increasing transparency regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics for global programs

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 46/50, IMANA shows high donor fit for the Muslim community by maintaining Zakat-eligible status and addressing urgent global health crises [2]. The organization provides multiple giving methods to support its mission of health maintenance in underserved communities worldwide [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: IMANA demonstrates a direct service model through programs such as ServeRohingya, ServePakistan, and ServeIndia, supported by a total revenue of $3,675,972 [1][3]. While the organization maintains a program expense ratio of 68.7%, the impact score of 32/50 reflects its role as a direct service provider while noting that specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not currently available [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Medical Association of North America (IMANA) provides critical global healthcare relief through a network of medical professionals and dedicated humanitarian programs.
strengths: Exceptional Charity Navigator score of 99.75/100 for accountability and finance
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.10 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 33.0 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: IMANA is a professional organization that fosters health promotion and disease prevention through direct patient care and international health programs [1]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various medical relief funds [2][4].

all_citations.claim: overall score of 99.99.75/100 from Charity Navigator
all_citations.quote: Overall Score 99.75/100. Accountability & Finance 99.75. Culture & Leadership 100.
IMANA is Zakat-eligible. Your Zakat donations support our medical relief funds.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMANA earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial accountability and strong alignment with Muslim donor values, though its impact score reflects a need for more granular cost-effectiveness data.

areas_for_improvement.context: While programs are verified, IMANA could improve by <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-per-beneficiary assessments.
With <cite id="1">33.0 months of operating reserves</cite>, the organization should publish a clear reserve-use policy to distinguish between restricted and operating funds.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains high accountability standards consistent with major rating agency requirements.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's <cite id="1">33.0 months of working capital</cite> provides a significant buffer against revenue fluctuations.
risk_factors: <cite id="3">Negative 3-year revenue growth of -21.3%</cite> indicates potential funding volatility.
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="1">lack of standardized cost-per-beneficiary data</cite>, which makes it difficult to compare its cost-effectiveness against other global health peers.

confidence.notes: Financial data is highly consistent across sources.

known_gaps: Missing specific beneficiary counts for cost-effectiveness analysis

alignment.explanation: The charity is a <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible organization</cite> serving <cite id="2">eight asnaf categories</cite> in high-urgency global health zones.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust IMANA due to its <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and <cite id="1">perfect scores in leadership and culture</cite> from third-party evaluators.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="1">standardize its reporting of outcome metrics</cite> across all international chapters.

impact.explanation: IMANA operates with a <cite id="3">68.7% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring nearly all funds reach medical relief efforts, supported by <cite id="3">$2.5 million in foreign grants</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact assessment would benefit from <cite id="1">tracking specific health outcomes</cite> rather than just output metrics like grant totals.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Verified outcomes with Platinum transparency

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds medical relief and development through domestic and international partners

rich_narrative.headline: The Islamic Medical Association of North America (IMANA) provides critical global healthcare relief through a network of medical professionals and dedicated humanitarian programs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support healthcare in the Muslim world through a professionally-led organization with exceptional financial accountability.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through medical relief
Value organizations led by healthcare professionals
Prioritize high program expense ratios and low overhead
Support both emergency relief and medical education
giving_considerations: Gifts support a mix of direct service and grants to other medical partners
The organization has a very large reserve, suggesting high financial security
Revenue has seen recent declines, making new donations critical for growth
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking small-scale, local community clinics in the US or those requiring rigorous RCT-level impact data.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: IMANA tracks <cite id="3">4 distinct outcome metrics</cite> and holds a Platinum seal for transparency, though cost-per-beneficiary data is missing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IMANA utilizes a network of medical professionals to provide direct care and grants to establish clinics, aiming to reduce disease and improve health maintenance globally.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that medical interventions are reaching the most vulnerable populations effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite strong reserves, a <cite id="3">-21.3% revenue CAGR</cite> suggests a need for increased donor support to sustain large-scale medical grants.
strategic_priorities: Deploy medical teams to refugee zones

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Gaza, Jordan, Yemen, Guyana, Pakistan, Nepal, Mauritania

peer_comparison.differentiator: IMANA's <cite id="3">68.7% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="3">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.

strengths.detail: IMANA holds a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 score for Culture & Leadership</cite> and an overall <cite id="1">99.75/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>.
The organization directs <cite id="3">68.7% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="3">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="1">33.0 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term sustainability for its global medical missions.

strengths_deep_dive: IMANA's operational strength is rooted in its ability to mobilize professional medical expertise at a low cost. The organization demonstrates <cite id="1">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="1">$0.13 to raise every dollar</cite>. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="3">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of recognition for nonprofit disclosure. Furthermore, its grantmaking capacity is significant, distributing <cite id="3">$3,833,988 in total grants</cite> to both domestic and foreign partners to expand healthcare access in underserved regions like <cite id="2">Gaza, Jordan, and Guyana</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Medical Association of North America (IMANA) is a professional and humanitarian organization that fosters health promotion and disease prevention through <cite id="1">direct patient care and international health programs</cite>. Operating as a network of medical professionals, IMANA leverages its expertise to provide <cite id="2">critical medical relief in regions including Gaza, Yemen, and Pakistan</cite>. The organization maintains a robust financial profile, reporting <cite id="3">$2,874,345 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. IMANA is highly regarded for its transparency and accountability, holding an <cite id="1">overall score of 99.99.75/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. Its operational model is characterized by high efficiency, with a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 68.7%</cite> and a fundraising efficiency of <cite id="1">$0.10 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond emergency relief, the organization supports long-term health infrastructure through <cite id="2">grants for clinics and hospitals</cite> and provides <cite id="1">accredited continuing medical education</cite> for healthcare providers. As a <cite id="2">zakat-eligible charity</cite>, IMANA offers Muslim donors a verified pathway to support global health initiatives that align with Islamic values of service and mercy.
